perturbation.original_text: (b) Licensee represents and warrants that: i. The execution, delivery and performance of this Agreement have been duly authorized by all necessary corporate action on the part of Licensee and this Agreement constitutes a valid and legally binding agreement of Licensee enforceable against Licensee in accordance with its terms; ii. It will use the Titles solely as permitted under this Agreement; iii. It has the full right, capacity and authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform all of its obligations hereunder; and iv. As of the Effective Date, there is no claim, action, suit, investigation or proceeding relating to or affecting Licensee pending or threatened, in law or in equity, or any other circumstance which might adversely affect Licensee's ability to perform all of its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Licensee represents and warrants that: i. The execution, delivery and performance of this Agreement have been duly authorized by all necessary corporate action on the part of Licensee and this Agreement constitutes a valid and legally binding agreement of Licensee enforceable against Licensee in accordance with its terms; ii. It will use the Titles in a manner broadly consistent with the permissions under this Agreement; iii. It has the full right, capacity and authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform all of its obligations hereunder, provided that unforeseen circumstances may limit Licensee's ability to fully perform such obligations; and iv. As of the Effective Date, there is no claim, action, suit, investigation or proceeding relating to or affecting Licensee pending or threatened, in law or in equity, or any other circumstance which might adversely affect Licensee's ability to perform all of its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying Licensee's warranties to be less stringent introduces ambiguity. Changing 'solely as permitted' to 'broadly consistent' and adding a caveat about unforeseen circumstances undermining 'full performance' introduces exceptions. This contradicts the initial warranty (i) that the agreement is 'legally binding' and 'enforceable in accordance with its terms' making the agreement less binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Licensee represents and warrants that: i. The execution, delivery and performance of this Agreement have been duly authorized by all necessary corporate action on the part of Licensee and this Agreement constitutes a valid and legally binding agreement of Licensee enforceable against Licensee in accordance with its terms; ii. It will use the Titles in a manner broadly consistent with the permissions under this Agreement; iii. It has the full right, capacity and authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform all of its obligations hereunder, provided that unforeseen circumstances may limit Licensee's ability to fully perform such obligations; and iv. As of the Effective Date, there is no claim, action, suit, investigation or proceeding relating to or affecting Licensee pending or threatened, in law or in equity, or any other circumstance which might adversely affect Licensee's ability to perform all of its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity and exceptions to the Licensee's warranties. Specifically, changing 'solely as permitted' to 'in a manner broadly consistent' and adding a caveat about 'unforeseen circumstances' undermining 'full performance' weakens the binding nature of the agreement. This directly contradicts the initial warranty (i) which states the agreement is 'legally binding' and 'enforceable in accordance with its terms.' The added ambiguity directly conflicts with the enforceability clause.

perturbation.original_text: Licensor will promptly provide written notice to Licensee in which Licensor lists each Additional Title. Should Licensee agree to be the vendor for an Additional Title, Licensor and Licensee will negotiate in good faith to mutually agree upon the pricing and terms for each Additional Title in an amendment to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensor will endeavor to provide written notice to Licensee in which Licensor lists each Additional Title. If Licensee expresses interest in being the vendor for an Additional Title, Licensor and Licensee will negotiate to potentially agree upon the pricing and terms for each Additional Title, subject to further considerations.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Licensor will promptly provide' to 'Licensor will endeavor to provide' weakens the obligation to provide notice for additional titles. Also, changing 'negotiate in good faith to mutually agree upon' to 'negotiate to potentially agree upon' introduces a caveat for mutual agreement. This creates uncertainty about whether Additional Titles will be offered to Licensee and if agreement terms are binding, contradicting the clear procedural intention in the original Section 5 and therefore also Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Additional Titles. If, during the Term, Licensor develops or obtains the rights to license any live action or animated feature-length motion picture (each an "Additional Title"), Licensor shall give Licensee the first right of negotiation for each Additional Title (i.e., the preferred vendor). Licensor will endeavor to provide written notice to Licensee in which Licensor lists each Additional Title. If Licensee expresses interest in being the vendor for an Additional Title, Licensor and Licensee will negotiate to potentially agree upon the pricing and terms for each Additional Title, subject to further considerations. Licensor will deliver each Additional Title in accordance with Section 4(a). Unless otherwise expressly stated in such an amendment, each Additional Title listed in such an amendment will be deemed a "Title" and Schedule A will be deemed amended to include such Additional Title.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Licensor 'will promptly provide' notice, creating a clear obligation. The modified text weakens this obligation to 'Licensor will endeavor to provide', introducing ambiguity. Additionally, changing 'negotiate in good faith to mutually agree upon' to 'negotiate to potentially agree upon' further weakens the obligation to reach a mutual agreement on terms. This directly contradicts the implied expectation in the 'Contradicted Text' that the Licensee has a 'first right of negotiation' and creates uncertainty about whether Licensor is obligated to offer and finalize terms for Additional Titles, which introduces an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Ambiguities' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Within fifteen (15) days after the Effective Date or on December 31, 2015 (whichever is earlier), Licensor shall (at Licensor's sole expense), make the Titles available either online or by hard drive to Licensee or the third-party vendor specified by Licensee to provide or deliver the Titles from Licensee's or its third-party vendor's facilities.
perturbation.changed_text: Within a reasonable timeframe after the Effective Date or around December 31, 2015 (whichever is earlier), Licensor shall make reasonable efforts (at Licensor's sole expense), to make the Titles available through methods deemed appropriate by Licensor to Licensee or the third-party vendor specified by Licensee to provide or deliver the Titles from Licensee's or its third-party vendor's facilities.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the specific delivery deadline ('fifteen (15) days') with a vague timeframe ('a reasonable timeframe') and softening the obligation ('shall' becomes 'shall make reasonable efforts') introduces significant uncertainty. This makes section 4(a) vague and less binding, where Section 7(b) allows for termination if there is a material breach and this weakens the definiton on when Licensor needs to deliver on that obligation. This therefore contradicts the clause 7(b) that allows for termination based on breach of the aggrement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) This Agreement may be terminated at any time by either party, effective immediately upon written notice, if the other party: (i) becomes insolvent; (ii) files a petition in bankruptcy; or (iii) makes an assignment for the benefit of its creditors. Either party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice if the other party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within thirty (30) days after the date that it receives written notice of such breach from the non-breaching party.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a specific deadline for delivery of titles (within 15 days or December 31, 2015), which creates a firm obligation for the licensor. The changed text introduces ambiguity by replacing the specific deadline with 'a reasonable timeframe' and weakens the obligation from 'shall' to 'shall make reasonable efforts.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, section 7(b), which allows for termination of the agreement if a party materially breaches the agreement and fails to cure the breach within 30 days. The vague language in the changed text makes it difficult to determine if a breach has occurred, creating uncertainty about when the Licensor needs to deliver. This uncertainty conflicts with the clear termination clause in Section 7(b) and weakens its enforceability, as it's unclear what constitutes a material breach related to delivery timeframe. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
